properties.Name: ASDAM
properties.Details: ASDAM is Australia’s largest supplier to the F-35 Joint Strike Fighter Program. An Australian advanced manufacturing and sustainment company, it provides end-to-end capability across design, engineering, manufacturing, assembly and sustainment. The group includes four subsidiaries: Marand, TAE Aerospace, Levett Engineering and Rosebank Engineering. ASDAM’s role is to provide continued access to capital and resources to build and grow Australian sovereign industrial capability. It is majority-owned by funds managed by CPE Capital. ASDAM’s members service a number of defence and non-defence programs, and have long term relationships with governments, defence prime contractors and other industrial customers. Customers include Lockheed Martin, BAE Systems, Honeywell, Pratt & Whitney and BHP. Combined, the four companies comprising ASDAM have 20 locations across Australia and the US and approx 1,000 employees. Headquarters (Marand’s address): 153 Keys Rd, Moorabbin, VIC, 3189. +61 (0)3 8552 0600 Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: AW Bell
properties.Details:
Involved in the F-35 program since 2007. It supplies casting and machining of three components of the chassis assembly to Northrop Grumman for the F-35’s ElectroOptical Distributed Aperture System (EODAS). The company also produces 19 different components for Marand for the F-35’s complex engine removal and installation trailer, which are precision-machined castings in a range of materials. It is also the sole approved casting supplier of F-35 flare magazines worldwide. Collaborates with Thales Australia, Rheinmetall Defence Australia to manufacture key components for Rheinmetall’s Mk30-2 cannon by Lithgow Arms for the ADF's Land 400 program. A.W. Bell are now applying its casting expertise and working directly with Rheinmetall to explore an increase in component manufacture for armoured vehicles in Australia. Key customers include: Northrop Grumman Corporation, BAE Systems Australia, Marand, Levett, Pratt & Whitney, Lockheed Martin (all F-35 program). (Source: Defence brochure 2018)
Headquarters: 145 Abbotts Road, Dandenong South, VIC 3175, Australia. +61 (0)3 9799 9555
Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: Axiom Precision Manufacturing
properties.Details: Contracted through BAE Systems to manufacture parts used in the vertical tail of the F-35. Axiom says it has delivered parts aligned to critical deadlines since 2015 at a high conformance and production rate. The components consist of four semi-finished and four finished parts that follow multiple operations, as well as different surface treatments and painting specifications.

properties.Name: Babcock
properties.Details:
Babcock makes it to this map because Babcock is up for serious involvement with the nuclear submarines and the Aukus programs. It claims decades of experience supporting the UK and the USA’s submarine fleets.

Babcock currently sustains 100 per cent of the UK’s Royal Navy submarine fleet and owns and operates the UK’s only licensed facility for refitting, refuelling and defueling nuclear submarines.

properties.Name: BAE Systems
properties.Details: Aviation maintenance facility, part of HMAS Albatross servicing Seahawk helicopters

Aircraft coating facility working on F-35 fleet, including internationally, cooperating with Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman at

properties.Name: BAE Systems

properties.Name: BAE Systems
properties.Details: One of the largest defence contractors in Australia and a key supplier to the F-35 program. Its UK parent, BAE Systems plc, is one of Lockheed Martin’s principal partners in the design, build and maintenance of the F-35. BAE Systems holds about 13-15 per cent work share globally of each F-35 aircraft. As the principal partner in the F-35 Program in Australia, BAE Systems Australia supports numerous Australian companies in the industry supply chain. It produces titanium components for the F-35 vertical tails and is also responsible for producing wiring boards and assemblies, cable assemblies, and selected electronic and communication components and other components

properties.Name: BAE Systems
properties.Details: BAE Systems Australia has a Melbourne-based aerospace facility where it designs advanced naval air defence systems like the Nulka Active Missile Decoy and the Evolved Sea Sparrow Missile System.

properties.Name: Beretta Australia
properties.Details: Small arms manufacturer. High security facility in Dandenong South is listed as their headquarters in Australia. Source: Beretta Australia

properties.Name: Bisalloy Steels
properties.Details: History of trade with isreali military companies. Signed defence contracts In 2017 and 2019 contracts with Rafael to provide ~200 tonnes of steel to make armoured fighting vehicles. Supplies military-grade steel for Plasan Re’em, an Israeli company making armoured cars used in occupied Palestine both by the military and the armed settler militias. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location. Ref: Bisalloy - Developing stronger ties with key export market

properties.Name: Cablex Pty Ltd
properties.Details:
Specialises in custom cables and electrical assemblies. For the F-35, the company provides semi-rigid radio frequency cables that enable communication between the radar and other elements of the F-35's avionics [Source, 2004]. Defence’s F-35 Australian industry participation charts (2017 and 2018) list Cablex as a supplier of ejection seat wiring, harnesses, radar, EW components and TPS development
Cablex received a three-year (2018-2021) $275,000 Australian government grant “to enable the manufacture in Australia of electrical assemblies for the F-35 Joint Strike Fighter Ejection Seat”. [Source].
Headquarters: 63-69 Fairbank Rd, Clayton South, VIC, 3169, +61 (0)3 9575 3088
Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

350 Parramatta Road
properties.Details: CAE AUSTRALIA: Defence lists CAE as having been involved in production and training studies for the (LM F35) JSF (Source: Defence graphic 2023). Despite it being a major supplier of training to the RAAF, I found no evidence that CAE Australia is involved in actual F-35 training. Other companies noted in this document have been named as providing F-35 training locally (Lockheed Martin, Milskil, KBR). Headquarters CAE: Building A, Campus Business Park, 350 Parramatta Road, Homebush NSW Australia 2140. +61-2-9748-4844. Offices also in Brisbane and Canberra. CAE Sale Military Training Centre: 17 Wellington Park Way, Sale, Victoria 3850, Australia
properties.Source: In 2022, Lockheed Martin was the world's largest arms-producing and military services company, with arms sales amounting to nearly 60 billion U.S. dollars. properties.Name: Electromold Australia
properties.Details:
A purpose-built processing and surface coating facility. It was acquired by Lovitt Technologies in 2013 to guarantee local supply of crucial wet processing and painting services. Electromold has worked on the F-35 program since early in the program’s inception in Australia. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location. [Sources: website and submission to JSF Inquiry] Headquarters: 202 Holt Parade, Thomastown, VIC, 3074. +61 (0)3 9464 0922
Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: EOS
properties.Details: Electro Optic Systems (EOS) is an Australian weapons manufacturer. In 2024 they sold their R400 remote weapons system to Israel and estimate potential sale of another 60. These systems can be fitting with 30mm cannon, machine gun, grenade launcher and anti-tank missiles. EOS describe the ability to fit a wide range of their weapons on the system as "plug & play" functionality. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: Ferra Engineering
properties.Details:
Every F-35 flying worldwide contains weapons adapters produced by Ferra Engineering (source). The company is the sole global provider of the Alternate Mission Equipment (AME) weapon adapters. In delivering this product, Ferra undertakes complex supply chain management and sub-assembly.
Ferra also designs, manufactures and assembles approximately 100 other parts for the F-35 fleet on long term contracts including complex mechanical assemblies. The parts are supplied either directly to Lockheed Martin or through other original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) for on-sale to Lockheed Martin. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location. (Source: Ferra submission to 2016 JSF Australian Senate inquiry.)

properties.Name: G&O Kert
properties.Details: Supplies avionics racks and components for the F-35 program (Source: Defence Graphic 2023). Industry partners include Ferra Engineering.

properties.Name: General Dynamics
properties.Details: General Dynamics has extensive business with 'Israel', including providing 120mm-calibre tank rounds to the IOF, as well as MK-84 2,000 pound bombs. General Dynamics supplies, upgrades and supports the ADF’s armoured fighting vehicle fleets.

properties.Name: Glyde Metal Industries (Zenith Group)
properties.Details: Glyde Metal provides laser cutting, steel fabrication and coating (under Glyde Metal) and defence and space manufacturing under Zenith Group. Supplies steel fabrication services for  Boeing, BAE, Rheinmetall, Ferra, Thyssenkrup, Rosebank Engineering (amoung others). Refs: Zenith Group, Glyde Metal

properties.Name: Hanwha
properties.Details: Hanwha has an ongoing active contract (2025) with 'Israeli' company Elbit Systems. Elbit will build automated turrets (canons) at Avalon. These weapons systems are one factor in a contract that Hanwha is coordinating for 129  Infantry Fighting Vehicle (IFV) ordered by the Dept of Defence. Orders like these on the face of it are for ADF equipment but may also be onsold as exports. Keep watch. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: Hayes Metals

properties.Name: Heat Treatment Australia
properties.Details: One of Australia’s leading heat treatment and thermal processing specialists. The company pioneered innovative processes for the F-35 program including a vacuum processing technique with high pressure gas quenching for the F-35’s landing gear components and new aluminium vacuum brazing (AVB) processes. HTA’s processes helped plug capability gaps and increase capacity in titanium processing, vacuum carburising, inert argon and nitrogen processing, nickel alloy and super-alloy processing and 25 bar materials cooling technology. The company’s support of RUAG, AW Bell, Lovitt Technologies, Marand, Ferra Engineering and Levett Engineering has contributed significantly to Australia’s success in the F-35 Program. Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers in Palestine

properties.Name: Heat Treatment Australia

properties.Name: HIFraser Group
properties.Details: Founded in Hobart in 1955, HIFraser is part of the Australian owned HIFraser Group, a combination of specialised engineering, manufacturing & instrumentation businesses. It produces and maintains niche safety critical gas and liquid systems and supplies ongoing repair and overhaul of ground support equipment. For the F-35, HIFraser has partnered with Lockheed Martin in the repair of air-to-air refuelling valves and fuel metering pumps, which allow the JSF to refuel in midair. The company is working on components for every JSF that comes off the assembly line, not only those of the RAAF. Refs: Who Arms Israel? Workers in Palestine, Daily Tele 2016

properties.Details: Supplies specialised production tooling to support the manufacture of metallic and composite components and aero structures, including the F-35, together with the machining of composite and metallic components.

properties.Details: IAI is owned by the 'israeli' government. They are one of three major 'israeli' offence industry companies contracted to provide the australian defence force with equipment and training. IAI has continued to profit from the sale of munitions since October 2023. Refs: DFAT, ArmyTechnology.com

properties.Name: KBR Australia
properties.Details: Areas of R&D: Innovative R&D for defence, HLS and emergency services: AI, robotics and autonomous systems, human machine teaming and training and simulation

properties.Name: Kongsberg
properties.Details:
Kongsberg (with Raytheon) is building the National Advanced Surface to Air Missile System for the Australian Army. The Australian government has given Kongsberg A$850 million to build Australia's first missile factory, at the Williamstown Aerospace Centre in Newcastle. It is due to be operational in 2026/27. Kongsberg also supplies advanced weapons systems to the Australian Defence Force (ADF), including:
* Naval Strike Missile (NSM): anti-ship and land-attack missile. 
* Joint Strike Missile (JSM): air-launched cruise missile, variant of the NSM, being integrated onto the RAAF's F-35A fighter aircraft.
* PROTECTOR Remote Weapon Systems (RWS): A family of remote-controlled weapon stations to be integrated on various mobile and static platforms.

properties.Name: L3 Harris
properties.Details: L3 Harris was named as a major supplier of weapons for the Israel Defence Force.

properties.Name: L3 Harris
properties.Details: L3 Harris was named as a major supplier of weapons for the Israel Defence Force.

properties.Name: L3 Micreo
properties.Details: Micreo, an Australian electronic warfare subsystems provider, was purchased by L3 Harris in 2016. A market leader in the design and production of radio frequency and photonic products for radar and electronic warfare. L3 Micreo’s radar components are installed in several of the world’s frontline military aircraft and ships, including the F-35.

properties.Name: Leidos
properties.Details: Leidos merged with Lockheed Martin's IT sector, Information Systems & Global Solutions (Lockheed Martin IS&GS), in August 2016 to create the defense industry’s largest IT services provider. The Leidos-Lockheed Martin merger is one of the biggest transactions thus far in the consolidation of the defense sector. Leidos contracts extensively with the Department of Defense, the Department of Homeland Security, and the Intelligence Community, as well as other U.S. government agencies and select commercial markets. Ref: Wiki 2024

properties.Name: Leidos
properties.Details: Leidos merged with Lockheed Martin's IT sector, Information Systems & Global Solutions (Lockheed Martin IS&GS), in August 2016 to create the defense industry’s largest IT services provider. The Leidos-Lockheed Martin merger is one of the biggest transactions thus far in the consolidation of the defense sector. Leidos contracts extensively with the Department of Defense, the Department of Homeland Security, and the Intelligence Community, as well as other U.S. government agencies and select commercial markets. Supplies to Pratt & Whitney, BAE Systems, Australian Department of Defence, Northrop Grumman, and Harris Corporation. Part of ASDAM.

properties.Name: Lintek
properties.Details: Using a patented high vacuum deposition process, Lintek manufactures a variety of printed circuit boards, ranging from 1.8-meter long antennas to sub miniature transmitters, using various substrates, for the phased array radar and electronic warfare systems of the F-35 program.

properties.Name: Lockheed Martin
properties.Details: Wholly-owned subsidiary of Lockheed Martin Corporation, the F-35’s prime contractor.

properties.Name: Lockheed Martin

properties.Name: Lockheed Martin

Arms sales constituted almost 90 percent of total company sales in that year. Unsurprisingly, Lockheed Martin supplies weapons to 'israel', including F-35 fighter jets. From their own website, they are proud to have been supplying C-130 and F-16 aircrafts to the 'israeli' air force since the 70s. Ref: Lockheed Martin

properties.Name: Lovitt Technologies
properties.Details: A leader in the manufacture of complex, precision engineered, structural components and assemblies for military aerospace platforms including the F-35. It specialises in aluminium and titanium components as well as chemical processing (through its subsidiary Electromold; see above). Lovitt supports a number of F-35-related activities for companies including Levett Engineering, Ferra Engineering, Marand, Quickstep, HTA, Varley and AW Bell. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location. (Sources: company website and Defence F-35 industry brochure, 2018)

properties.Name: Marand
properties.Details: One of the most successful Australian companies in the F-35 supply chain, receiving about one third of the Australian contracts in the program. Marand designed and manufactures the F-35’s engine removal and installation trailer and is recognised globally as its Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM). The company also has a long-term agreement with BAE Systems for the manufacture of the aircraft’s vertical tails, one of the largest planned manufacturing projects for the F-35 in Australia (Source, 2014). In 2016 Marand also won the detailed design and manufacture contract for the Engine/Lift Fan Removal and Installation Trainer (ELFRIT), and the company also supplies tooling to BAE Systems in the UK and four other F-35 partner nations. Marand has nine Australian companies directly involved in its F-35 program supply chain, with several others enabling its manufacturing efforts. Marand is part of ASDAM. Headquarters: 153 Keys Rd, Moorabbin, VIC, 3189. +61 (0)3 8552 0600 Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: Marand
properties.Details: One of the most successful Australian companies in the F-35 supply chain, receiving about one third of the Australian contracts in the program. properties.Name: Martin Baker Australia
properties.Details: Subsidiary of British firm Martin Baker, specialises in ejection seats and related equipment. In Australia, focuses on maintenance, repair, and overhaul of ejector seats for F-35s. Partnered with BAE Systems Australia for repairs of F-35 life support systems, including oxygen generator and ejection seat assembly.

properties.Name: Milskil
properties.Street Address: Unit 2.01, Level 1, Building E, 1 Technology Place
properties.Details: Provides training development and delivery, including aircrew training instruction for the F-35A. Teamed with Lockheed Martin Australia for a 3-year, $67m Defence contract to deliver F-35 training services. Includes pilot instructors, maintenance instructors, courseware developers, and IT support, plus operation and maintenance support for F-35A full mission simulators.

properties.Name: NIOA
properties.Details: Produces munitions, from small arms to missiles. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: NIOA

Produces 25mm Frangible Armour Piercing (FAP) projectiles at the federal Government-owned Benalla plant in Victoria in a joint venture with Rheinmetall (Source: Media Release: Press Release). Also manufactures a range of munitions for the Australian Defence Force and for export.

properties.Name: NIOA
properties.Details: Nioa is a family trust type Prime weapons dealer. Nioa has a lot of projects: supplier ammunition and guns for Aus police. Owner of US based Barret that makes sniper rifles for the IDF. 50% Owner of the 155mm shells factory in Maryborough, QLD: exporting all to Germany. Producer of ammunition at Benalla, Victoria.

properties.Name: QINETIQ Australia
properties.Details: Subsidiary of British arms company QinetiQ. Provides training support to the Australian Defence Force on platforms such as the F-35.

properties.Name: Quickstep Holdings Limited
properties.Details: Australia's largest independent aerospace-grade advanced composite manufacturer. Key supplier to Northrop Grumman, providing 21 components for the F-35’s fuselage. Supplies Marand Precision Engineering with parts for the F-35 vertical tail. Delivered its 10,000th F-35 component in December 2020. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: Raytheon
properties.Details: Joint Adversarial Training & Testing (JATTS) program providing jobs for over 100 people in the region. This team deliver airborne electronic warfare, aerial opposing force, aerial targets, and training and testing services to the ADF.

properties.Name: RFD Australia
properties.Details: Australian division of Survitec, specializes in the supply and service of survival technology, including Aircrew Flight Equipment (AFE) for each F-35 pilot. Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers in Palestine

properties.Name: Rheinmetall
properties.Details: Munition Testing

properties.Name: Rheinmetall/NIOA
properties.Details: Joint venture between Rheinmetall Waffe Munition GmbH (51%) and NIOA (49%). Manufactures 155mm bombs. Produces and exports ammunition for the F-35 JSF program. Produces 25mm Frangible Armour Piercing (FAP) projectiles at the federal Government-owned Benalla plant in Victoria (Source: Media Release: Press Release). Australian-owned NIOA is the leading supplier of weapons and munitions to the Australian Defence Force and Rheinmetall Waffe Munitions is a global multinational weapons manufacturer. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: Rockwell Collins Australia
properties.Details: A wholly-owned subsidiary of Collins Aerospace, a US subsidiary of the RTX corporation (formerly Raytheon Technologies). Manufactures the electro-optical (EO) assembly from the AAQ-37 Distributed Aperture System for the F-35. 8 locations in Australia.

properties.Name: Ronson Gears
properties.Details: Flight specialists who produce precision gear components and control systems including for the F-35 Engine Removal and Installation Mobility Trailer, which was designed and developed by Melbourne-based Marand Precision Engineering. Clients including, BAE Systems, Marand, and Ferra Engineering. Headquarters: 18 Teton Court, Highett, Victoria, 3190. +61 3 9276 8900

properties.Name: Rosebank Engineering
properties.Details: ROSEBANK ENGINEERING (formerly RUAG Australia) started its involvement in the F-35 program in 2004 with the manufacture of 30 components for the weapons bay door drive system uplocks. Rosebank’s participation in the program has since expanded significantly. It produces and sustains airframe components and actuation systems, and manages sustainment of engine and fuel components. (Source: Defence brochure 2023) Rosebank is a sole source supplier to the F-35 program providing over 150 components for the landing gear and weapons bay systems on all three variants. Its uplock actuator system opens and closes the weapons bay doors in seconds enabling the aircraft to drop its payload of munitions quickly to maintain its stealth capacity. In 2014, Rosebank opened a metal processing and finishing facility in Wingfield (northern Adelaide) to treat and finish the F-35 vertical tail components manufactured by Marand for BAE Systems. These components require specialised capabilities and can only be processed at the Wingfield facility. (Source: Defence F-35 industry brochure, 2018; ABC, 2013) In October 2023, Rosebank announced the activation of its F-35 Wheels & Brakes Repair Depot at Bayswater in Melbourne. Operating under sub-contract to prime contractor Lockheed Martin, Rosebank is the preferred component maintenance, repair, overhaul & upgrade (CMRO&U) assignee on five repair technology groups (RTGs) of the F-35 platform for the Indo-Pacific region. Rosebank is part of ASDAM. 5 locations in Australia. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location. Headquarters: 836 Mountain Hwy, Bayswater, VIC 3153. +61 (0)3 9721 1300 Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: SEC Plating
properties.Details: Manufactures plating, engineering coatings and finishes for the F-35. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: SMASH Australia
properties.Details: Subsidiary of Smartshooter, an 'israeli' arms company. Specialises in remote weapons stations and fire control systems, including remote-controlled gun turrets that can be affixed to military vessels as well as remote weapons with integrated radars.

properties.Name: Sutton Tools
properties.Details: Supplies its cutting tools to BAE Systems Australia and other JSF manufacturing companies. (Source: DMTC submission to JSF Senate Inquiry) Headquarters: 378 Settlement Road, Thomastown, VIC, 3074. +61 (0)3 9280 0800

properties.Name: TAE Aerospace
properties.Details: Provides aerospace manufacturing and aluminium vacuum brazing capabilities to support parts and component manufacture for the global F-35 fleet. The company established an in-house aluminium vacuum brazing design capability allowing it to move further up the supply chain...Significantly, TAE Aerospace now provides a complete repair, overhaul and test facility for military aircraft, including all F-35s operating in the Asia-Pacific (source: company website). The company’s F135 maintenance, repair, overhaul and upgrade (MRO&U) facility in Ipswich, Queensland is the first operational F135 engine depot in the Asia-Pacific region

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Munitions production

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: The factory now designs and manufactures military-grade rifles used by Australian forces (EF88)

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Providing asset management services in support of the Huon Class Minehunter fleet & Ship Repair/Slipway Services. Will be developed into autonomous maritime (i.e. drone boat) facilities

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Service hub and research facility, particularly for Tiger helicopters and general avionics

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Rigid Hull Inflatable Boat Maintenance, local warehousing, materials management and distribution for Darwin home ported or visiting ships, both warships and commercial vessels

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Explosive ordance depot at RAAF base at Darwin

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Maintenance Contracts

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Thales Australia builds wheeled armoured vehicles at Bendigo: The Bushmaster and the Hawkei. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Thales Australia is the largest supplier of explosive ordnance to the Australian Defence Force. The company’s Benalla facility in conjunction with Mulwala produces a wide range of ammunition, explosive ordnance and other munitions. As of 2025, individual tactics or campaigns have commenced at this location.

properties.Name: Thales
properties.Details: Thales Australia is the largest supplier of explosive ordnance to the Australian Defence Force. The company’s Benalla facility in conjunction with Mulwala produces a wide range of ammunition, explosive ordnance and other munitions.

properties.Name: Tr Calibration
properties.Details: Specialises in the calibration and repair of test and measurement equipment and instruments within Australia. TR Calibration is part of TR Group, Australia’s leading provider of technology solutions. Its customers include Raytheon Australia, Australian Defence Force, New Zealand Defence Force, Boeing Defence Australia, Airbus, BAE Systems, Lockheed Martin. Ref: Who arms Israel? Workers in Palestine

properties.Name: Trimcast
properties.Details:
A subsidiary of Pelican Products specialising in protective packaging. It is the major case supplier to the Australian Defence Force. Provides storage containers for the F-35 program. (Source: Defence graphic, 2017)
Australian head office: 2 Forbes Close, Knoxfield, VIC, 3180 +61 (03) 9765 1500
Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: Ubeeco Packaging Solutions
properties.Details: Provides shipping containers and packaging for aerospace and defence companies. Known as Integra Packaging before 2021.

properties.Name: Uneek Bending Co
properties.Details: Has utilised its design, metal shaping and fabrication expertise in a variety of defence based projects including the F-35 joint strike fighter. The company specialises in bespoke solutions for complex metal structures that require purpose shaping and bending for specific applications. Headquarters: 63 Mark Anthony Drive, Dandenong South, VIC, 3175. +61 3 9771 4900 Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: Varley Group
properties.Details: Involved in the F-35 program from 2003 during the System Development and Demonstration phase. The company has since been given numerous contracts to deliver specialised F-35 aircraft ground support equipment for all three F-35 variants. Varley works with both Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman on the program. It designed and manufactures the landing gear handling system and canopy maintenance sling assembly for Lockheed Martin, achieving the status of original equipment manufacturer (OEM) for these items. In 2008, Varley was also contracted by LM to design and develop a field use variant of the landing gear handling system in addition to the original variant used at military bases. In 2015, Varley signed a long term agreement with Lockheed Martin. For Northrop Grumman, from 2005, Varley designed and has manufactured the F-35’s aircraft maintenance sling.

properties.Name: Varley Group
properties.Details: Involved in the F-35 program from 2003 during the System Development and Demonstration phase. The company has since been given numerous contracts to deliver specialised F-35 aircraft ground support equipment for all three F-35 variants. Varley works with both Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman on the program. It designed and manufactures the landing gear handling system and canopy maintenance sling assembly for Lockheed Martin, achieving the status of original equipment manufacturer (OEM) for these items. In 2008, Varley was also contracted by LM to design and develop a field use variant of the landing gear handling system in addition to the original variant used at military bases. In 2015, Varley signed a long term agreement with Lockheed Martin. For Northrop Grumman, from 2005, Varley designed and has manufactured the F-35’s aircraft maintenance sling.

properties.Details: Involved in the F-35 program from 2003 during the System Development and Demonstration phase. properties.Name: Varley Group / Varley Rafael Australia
properties.Details:
Produce the SPIKE range of Precision Guided Tactical Missiles (in Australia it appears only for the ADF)
It is not definite that VRA works out of the Moorabbin site, but all evidence points toward that being the most likely site. Refs: Varley Group - Site Locations, VRA Systems

properties.Name: Vipac Engineers
properties.Details: Involved in the F-35A’s airframe structural design and finite element (FE) analysis and testing (source: company video). It also partnered with BAE Systems to optimise machining efficiency (source: DMTC submission to JSF Senate Inquiry). 4 locations in Australia. Headquarters: 279 Normanby Rd, Port Melbourne, VIC, 3207. +61 (0)3 9647 9700 Ref: Who Arms Israel?, Workers for Palestine

properties.Name: Western Australia Specialty Alloys (Wasa)
properties.Details: Specialises in materials supply (titanium, nickel, aluminium, and steel alloys) and supplies the master remelt alloy barstock for the PWA powder atomisation process used to produce critical components for the F-35. Customers include Pratt & Whitney, Rolls Royce, and GE Aviation.
